qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Any authenticated user can read arbitrary attachments by enumerating `file_id` in `view.php`
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an authorization flaw in `view.php` attachment retrieval by `file_id`, not the advisory's SQL query concatenation in the `get_sedi` handler.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Direct user-management action endpoint lets any authenticated user administer other accounts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a missing authorization check in `modules/utenti/actions.php`, not the advisory's SQL injection in the Anagrafiche AJAX completion query.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public OAuth initiation endpoints let unauthenticated users wipe stored OAuth tokens
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is public token invalidation in `oauth2.php` and `oauth2_login.php`, not the advisory's `idanagrafica` SQL injection in `get_sedi`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause. The advisory is an authenticated SQL injection in the `get_sedi` branch of the Anagrafiche AJAX completion handler where `idanagrafica` is concatenated into a SQL query, while the qualifying findings cover an attachment IDOR, a user-management authorization bypass, and a public OAuth token wipe.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes SQL injection in `ajax_complete.php` / `modules/anagrafiche/ajax/complete.php` for `op=get_sedi`, where attacker-controlled `idanagrafica` is concatenated into a query executed by `$dbo->fetchArray($q)`.
The qualifying finding `arbitrary_attachment_read_by_file_id` targets `view.php` attachment disclosure by enumerating `file_id`, not SQL construction or the `get_sedi` handler.
The qualifying finding `authenticated_admin_action_bypass` targets skipped permission checks in `modules/utenti/actions.php`, not the advisory's vulnerable SQL query path.
The qualifying finding `unauthenticated_oauth_token_wipe` targets unauthenticated OAuth token nulling in `oauth2.php` and `oauth2_login.php`, not the advisory sink.
A search across the current session artifacts did not find any qualifying finding report referencing `get_sedi`, `idanagrafica`, `ajax_complete.php`, or the advisory's SQL injection path.
